title: Pre-release security review (multi-agent)
description: Full security review before public release. Multi-agent fan-out across auth, MCP surface, web routes, secrets/config, data access, dependencies.
close_reason: Audit complete. Filed 18 follow-up issues: 3 P0 (ship blockers), 5 P1, 8 P2, 2 P3. Synthesis posted in conversation; full per-agent reports archived in session transcript.

title: If we add user signup, put it at /signup not /register (claimed by markland-6o6)
description:
markland-6o6 mounted a JSON-404 handler at /register for OAuth dynamic-client-registration probe coverage. The natural symmetric pair to /login is /register, so a future user signup page would naturally live there.

Decision: future signup goes at /signup (or /join), not /register. The OAuth probe handler is more load-bearing (every Claude Code MCP install hits it).

If we ever change our mind: this route must be removed first, then signup can take over the path. The OAuth probe coverage would need to move elsewhere (no obvious clean alternative — /register is what the SDK probes by spec).

Comment in src/markland/web/well_known_routes.py near the @app.api_route('/register') decorator flags this.

title: SECURITY P0: JS-context XSS in invite/settings_agents/device templates
description:
Jinja autoescape does not cover JS string contexts. Three sites:

1. templates/invite.html:28,30 - {{ token }} and {{ doc_share_token }} inside JS string literals
2. templates/settings_agents.html:51 - onsubmit confirm() with {{ a.display_name }} (user-controlled, can break out via apostrophe)
3. templates/device.html:25 - {{ code }} from query string into href attribute

Fix: use {{ value | tojson }} for JS contexts; remove inline confirm() in favor of data-* attribute + JS handler.

Found by: pre-release multi-agent security review (markland-06e)

title: SECURITY P3: uvicorn.error logger propagates without log scrubbing
description:
From PR #62 code review (F2). build_uvicorn_log_config sets propagate=False on uvicorn.access (good) but leaves uvicorn.error defaulting to True. If uvicorn logs a request URL at error level (rare), it bypasses redact filter and lands in JSON root handler unscrubbed.

Fix: install redact filter on root handler too, OR set propagate=False on uvicorn.error and give it its own filter-equipped handler.

Files: src/markland/log_scrubbing.py:111-117, src/markland/run_app.py:41-43.

Found by: code review of markland-ofj / PR #62.

title: Author /about-the-author page + Person schema
description:
From SEO strategy (Appendix C item 3). E-E-A-T building.

Currently: footer byline links to github.com/dghiles, Organization.founder JSON-LD has {@type: Person, name: '@dghiles', url: github}. That's a thin author signal.

Build:
1. New page at /authors/dghiles (or /about/dghiles — TBD per site URL convention; /about/dghiles probably reads better):
   - H1: real name OR 'Davey Hiles' OR whatever the public-facing identity will be (note from docs/FOLLOW-UPS.md: 'real name vs handle' is an open question — resolve before shipping).
   - 200-300w bio: who you are, what you've built before, why Markland.
   - Person JSON-LD: name, alternateName ('@dghiles'), jobTitle, image, sameAs (github, linkedin once exists, anything else relevant).
   - Links to: Markland (the project), GitHub, any other public work.
2. Update Organization.founder to reference this Person via @id (linked-data style):
   '@id': 'https://markland.dev/about/dghiles#person'
   The Person on /about/dghiles has the matching @id.
3. Author byline on every blog post (/blog/{slug}) links to /about/dghiles.
4. Test: tests/test_author_page.py asserts page renders, has Person schema with required fields, footer links resolve.

Coordinate timing with Phase 2.1 (/blog infra) — they should ship together so the first blog post can link to a real author page rather than a github profile.

Effort: 1 hour (mostly writing the bio + deciding the name question).

title: SECURITY P3: dependency hygiene (floor pins, action SHAs, litestream checksum)
description:
Multiple supply-chain hardening items:

1. All 12 prod deps in pyproject.toml use floor pins (>=) only. uv.lock provides reproducibility but uv lock --upgrade is unguarded. Recommend ~= or exact pins on a quarterly bump cadence.

2. .github/workflows/*.yml pin actions by major tag (@v4, @v1, @v3) instead of commit SHA.

3. Dockerfile line 19-21 downloads litestream tarball from GitHub without SHA-256 verification.

4. Base image python:3.12-slim not digest-pinned.

Found by: pre-release multi-agent security review (markland-06e)

title: SECURITY P2: share-token-as-capability for private docs (revoke-grant doesn't revoke URL)
description:
GET /d/{share_token} for non-public docs grants permanent view access independent of grant table. Revoking a grant does not revoke the share URL.

Fix: rotate share_token on grant-revoke, OR restrict /d/{share_token} to is_public=1 and route signed-in viewers through /api/docs/{id} only.

Files: src/markland/web/app.py:594

Found by: pre-release multi-agent security review (markland-06e)

title: MCP /mcp endpoint should advertise auth model (WWW-Authenticate + RFC 9728 metadata)
description:
Today daveyhiles@gmail.com hit a confusing failure when registering Markland in Claude Code without --header. Symptom in /mcp UI:

  Status: failed
  SDK auth failed: HTTP 404: Invalid OAuth error response:
  SyntaxError: JSON Parse error: Unrecognized token '<'.
  Raw body: <!DOCTYPE html> ...

Root cause:
1. /mcp returns 401 {error:unauthenticated} with NO WWW-Authenticate header.
2. Per MCP authorization spec (2025-03-26) + RFC 9728, the SDK probes /.well-known/oauth-protected-resource as a fallback.
3. That path falls through to Markland's styled HTML 404 page (Content-Type: text/html).
4. SDK tries to JSON-parse the HTML, crashes on '<'.

Verified via curl on 2026-05-03:
- POST /mcp without auth → 401 application/json {error:unauthenticated} (no WWW-Authenticate)
- GET /.well-known/oauth-protected-resource → 404 text/html
- GET /.well-known/oauth-authorization-server → 404 text/html

Fix options (smallest first):

A. Add 'WWW-Authenticate: Bearer realm="markland", resource_metadata="https://markland.dev/.well-known/oauth-protected-resource"' to the 401 response on /mcp. Even without serving the metadata doc, this signals the auth scheme.

B. Serve /.well-known/oauth-protected-resource per RFC 9728 with a JSON body that documents the bearer-token requirement and points users at /settings/tokens. Returning JSON (even a stub) instead of HTML stops the SDK from crashing on '<'.

C. Serve /.well-known/oauth-authorization-server returning 404 with Content-Type: application/json + a small body. Also stops the parse error if the SDK probes there.

Recommend A+B together: header tells the SDK 'here's where to look,' the metadata doc tells it 'no OAuth, use a static bearer.' Some MCP clients still won't handle 'no OAuth allowed' gracefully, but at least the failure is explicit instead of a parse error on HTML.

Acceptance:
- Register Markland in Claude Code without --header.
- /mcp UI should show a meaningful 'no auth configured' or similar message, not 'JSON Parse error: Unrecognized token <'.
- Existing --header registrations continue to work unchanged.

Related: docs/runbooks/admin-operations.md and seed-content/admin/07-quickstart-claude-code.md (already updated locally to mention --header) need to also tell users that --header is REQUIRED, not optional.
close_reason: Server advertises bearer auth via WWW-Authenticate header on /mcp 401 + RFC 9728 /.well-known/oauth-protected-resource (200 JSON) and /.well-known/oauth-authorization-server (404 JSON). MCP clients no longer fall through to HTML 404 + JSON.parse crash. Production-verified at https://markland.dev. Quickstart doc 3366aa58f6ead5e7 republished with corrected --transport http --scope user --header install command. PR #66.

title: Fix copy-token UX: shortcut + don't disappear
description: settings_tokens.html reloads 500ms after showing the new token, wiping it before user can copy. Add Copy button + keyboard shortcut, auto-select on display. Same affordance on settings_agents.html for the agent-token flash.
close_reason: Shipped in PR #65 (commit ab22712). Auto-deploys to Fly via deploy.yml on push to main.

title: Anchor blog post #1: "What is agent-native publishing?"
description:
From SEO strategy (Appendix C item 2). The first of 6 anchor posts.

Goal: define the term 'agent-native publishing' on the open web. Today no other site uses the phrase; ranking page-one for it within 90 days is realistic.

Target queries: 'agent-native publishing', 'agent-native', 'publishing for AI agents'.

Outline:
1. Hook: paste of an actual Claude Code session ending with the agent producing a markdown spec, and the awkward copy-paste-into-Slack moment.
2. Definition (134-167 words for AI-citation): 'Agent-native publishing is X' — three-clause, lead with what it IS not what it is not. Mention markdown, MCP, share link, three-way handoff (H2H/M2M/H2M).
3. Why it matters now: the rise of MCP-compatible clients in 2026 (Claude Code, Cursor, Codex, Continue) means agent output volume is exploding but no platform was designed for share-from-the-agent flow.
4. What it is NOT: not Notion (block model), not Google Docs (rich text), not Git (repo as unit), not a paste site (no agent surface).
5. The Markland take (soft pitch): markland_publish call → URL → done.
6. Outbound: link to /alternatives, /quickstart, and one external 'further reading' (e.g., Anthropic MCP docs).

Word count target: 1200-1800 (long enough to be substantive, short enough to actually read).

Style: developer-to-developer, first-person OK ('I built Markland because...'), avoid marketing voice. No bullet-point spam — use prose with deliberate H2/H3 structure for citation surface.

SEO checklist before publishing:
- Title: 'What is agent-native publishing? A definition for the MCP era' (~60 chars)
- Meta description: 140-160 chars, lead with definition
- Slug: /blog/agent-native-publishing
- OG image (use claude-seo:seo-image-gen or hand-roll)
- Article JSON-LD fully populated
- 1 internal link per 200 words minimum
- Cross-post: dev.to (with canonical), HN as Show HN, r/ClaudeAI

Effort: 3 hours writing + 30 min publishing flow.
close_reason: Anchor post #1 'What is agent-native publishing?' shipped in PR #63 alongside the blog infra. Live at https://markland.dev/blog/agent-native-publishing — 1403 words, 155-char meta description, definition lead in 134-167w AI-citation window. Two factual claims caught in code review (tool count + fork API) corrected before merge.

title: Nav 'Sign in' link drops return path
description: Anonymous viewer of /d/{share_token} clicks 'Sign in' in the nav (_signed_in_nav.html). The link is bare /login with no next=, so after magic-link verify they land on /verify_sent ('You're signed in'), not back on the doc. Reproduced with noahpan323@gmail.com sharing on 2026-05-04: he authed but couldn't get back to the share. Fix: pass next=request.url.path (URL-encoded) so /verify redirects to the share path.

title: scripts/admin/revoke_admin_token.py for end-to-end revocation
description: Runbook line 61 references 'list_admin_tokens.py followed by a DELETE' but neither a revoke script nor a documented DELETE form exists. One-liner: UPDATE tokens SET revoked_at=? WHERE id=?. Reviewer flagged in PR #57.

title: mint_admin_token.py: deterministic admin selection
description: scripts/admin/mint_admin_token.py:29 picks 'WHERE is_admin=1 LIMIT 1' with no ORDER BY — non-deterministic on multi-admin instances. Either ORDER BY created_at ASC and update docstring to 'oldest admin', or accept --user <email> with fallback. Reviewer flagged in PR #57.

title: Implement formal Terms of Service (plan 2026-05-04)
description: Execute docs/plans/2026-05-04-formal-terms-of-service.md — promote /terms to standard-shaped 14-section ToS. Anonymize: replace dghiles with magic_davey.
close_reason: Both formal trust-page rewrites landed: /privacy (12 tasks) and /terms (15 tasks). Word-count floors bumped (800/900), ROADMAP updated. Email-alias provisioning tracked as separate beads follow-ups.

title: SECURITY P3: markdown link allowlist permits protocol-relative // URLs
description:
From PR #62 code review (F1). Allowlist allows empty-scheme URLs (relative paths) but [click](//evil.com/x) produces href=//evil.com/x which the browser treats as https://evil.com/x. Open-redirect / phishing vector — no JS execution.

Fix: in src/markland/web/renderer.py:_validate_link, add: if url.startswith('//'): return False.

Found by: code review of markland-y2y / PR #62.

title: SECURITY P3: visibility flip (public→private) should rotate share_token
description:
Sibling of markland-1e8 (PR #64). set_visibility(public→private) does not rotate share_token, so anyone who saved the public URL retains view access after the doc goes private. Same severity class as P2-F.

Fix: in service/docs.py:set_visibility, when transitioning public→private, call db.rotate_share_token(doc_id).

Found by: code review of PR #64.

title: MCP SDK still trips HTML 404 on /register + /.well-known/openid-configuration variants
description:
Follow-up to markland-2yj. The first fix made /.well-known/oauth-protected-resource and /.well-known/oauth-authorization-server return JSON. Production logs from 2026-05-04 show Claude Code's MCP SDK ALSO probes (all on every UI panel open):

- POST /register                                         (RFC 7591 dynamic client registration)
- GET /.well-known/openid-configuration
- GET /.well-known/openid-configuration/mcp
- GET /.well-known/oauth-authorization-server/mcp
- GET /.well-known/oauth-protected-resource/mcp
- GET /mcp/.well-known/openid-configuration

All return Markland's styled HTML 404. The SDK's JSON.parse crashes on '<', leaves auth state in limbo, and the MCP `initialize` handshake never completes — so no mcp__markland__* tools surface in user sessions even though `claude mcp list` reports ✓ Connected.

Reproduced 2026-05-04 with daveyhiles@gmail.com's install: bearer header is in ~/.claude.json, JSON-RPC POST /mcp/ returns 200 OK, but tools/list never resolves and `markland_whoami()` reports tool-not-available.

Fix: extend register_well_known_routes to mount uniform JSON 404 handlers at every observed probe path. Add an integration test that hits each path and asserts JSON content-type. Deploy and re-verify that a real Claude Code install completes initialize and surfaces tools.
close_reason: All observed Claude Code MCP SDK probe paths now return JSON 404 (or 401, 200) instead of HTML. Production-verified at https://markland.dev — every path in the SDK's discovery probe sequence returns application/json. PR #68 (commit 255fc7b). Live now; user should restart Claude Code and confirm /mcp panel shows ✓ connected ✓ authenticated and markland_whoami() returns principal info.

title: Bound rate-limiter hit dicts in device_routes.py to prevent slow leak
description: src/markland/web/device_routes.py: _device_start_hits and _device_confirm_hits (defaultdict(deque) keyed by IP) never GC empty entries — slow leak in long-running processes bounded by distinct source-IP cardinality. Two cheap remedies: (1) periodic prune dropping keys whose deques are empty AND whose last hit was > 1 window ago; (2) cachetools.TTLCache. Not launch-blocking; flagged in PR #49 review.

title: Implement formal Privacy Policy (plan 2026-05-04)
description: Execute docs/plans/2026-05-04-formal-privacy-policy.md — promote /privacy to standard-shaped 10-section policy. Anonymize: replace dghiles with magic_davey.
close_reason: Both formal trust-page rewrites landed: /privacy (12 tasks) and /terms (15 tasks). Word-count floors bumped (800/900), ROADMAP updated. Email-alias provisioning tracked as separate beads follow-ups.

title: SECURITY P1: public-doc presence leaks viewer identity to anonymous readers
description:
GET /d/{share_token} renders presence list with principal_id, display_name, and free-text note - to ANY holder of the share token, including anonymous visitors on public docs. Internal user_ids exposed.

Fix: strip principal_id from anonymous responses; for unauthenticated viewers either suppress presence or show count only.

Files: src/markland/web/app.py:594-633

Found by: pre-release multi-agent security review (markland-06e)

title: SECURITY P3: usr_pending_<hash> shape distinguishable by length from real usr_ IDs
description:
From PR #64 fixup review. Synthetic principal_id for invite-pending grants is 'usr_pending_<16hex>' (length 28) vs real user IDs 'usr_<16hex>' (length 20). An attacker who has seen real grant responses elsewhere can distinguish on length alone.

More conservative fix: mint 'usr_<16hex>' with a domain-separated hash so length matches real IDs.

Found by: code review of PR #64 fixup.

title: SECURITY P2: rate-limit middleware trusts client X-Forwarded-For first hop
description:
src/markland/web/rate_limit_middleware.py:62-66 + src/markland/web/device_routes.py:108-112 read first XFF token unconditionally. On Fly the proxy sets it correctly but app does not validate. Fix: trust Fly-Client-IP on Fly (Fly overwrites every hop), fall back to request.client.host otherwise.

Found by: pre-release multi-agent security review (markland-06e)

title: Evaluate publishing Markland as a Claude Code plugin (/plugins Discover surface)
description:
Currently Markland is installed via 'claude mcp add --transport http markland https://markland.dev/mcp'. The /plugins surface in Claude Code is a separate distribution channel — users browse marketplaces and install plugins that bundle commands/agents/hooks/MCP configs.

Decision deferred: stay on MCP-only install for now. Revisit if we see signal that:
- Users are hitting friction typing the mcp add command
- Quickstart doc bounce rate is high
- We accumulate slash commands or subagents worth bundling alongside the MCP server

If we do this, MVP shape:
- New repo (e.g. github.com/markland-dev/claude-plugin) with .claude-plugin/plugin.json + marketplace.json + .mcp.json pointing at https://markland.dev/mcp
- Users install via 'claude plugin marketplace add markland-dev/claude-plugin'
- Then Markland shows up in /plugins Discover

Trigger: 50+ active MCP installs OR a community marketplace asks to list us, whichever first.

title: SECURITY P2: O(N) Argon2 verify scan on every Bearer-authed request
description:
src/markland/service/auth.py:158-234 resolve_token iterates all non-revoked tokens running argon2 verify per row. DoS lever; rate-limited at 20/min anon but distributed attacker can stall workers.

Fix: include non-secret token-id prefix (mk_usr_<tokid>_<random>) so row is fetchable in O(1).

Already noted as Plan 10 work in the docstring.

Found by: pre-release multi-agent security review (markland-06e)

title: Dedup admin handler auth: /admin/waitlist and /admin/metrics still re-resolve bearer
description: After PR #49 (security follow-ups batch), PrincipalMiddleware gates the entire /admin/ prefix, but admin_waitlist (app.py:426-471) and admin_metrics (app.py:473-488) still call header.lower().startswith('bearer ') + resolve_token themselves. This is a wasted DB lookup per request and dead code that contradicts the Task 6 refactor. Replace both with: principal = getattr(request.state, 'principal', None); 401 if None; 403 if not is_admin.
close_reason: shipped independently via PR #50 (audit L4 admin dedup)

title: G5: Drop /explore from sitemap.xml until it has content
description:
From 2026-05-03 GEO analysis (item G5).

/explore currently renders 73 words (just the H1 + a 'no public docs yet' state). It's in https://markland.dev/sitemap.xml so Google will eventually flag it as 'Crawled — currently not indexed', dragging down aggregate site quality signals.

Two options:

A. Inject a 200w evergreen lead-in describing what the explore feed surfaces and why public docs are worth browsing. Keep in sitemap.

B. (PREFERRED) Pull /explore out of build_sitemap_xml() until there are >= N (5?) public docs. Add the URL back conditionally.

Implementation (option B):
1. Edit src/markland/web/seo.py — build_sitemap_xml() takes a list of urls; the caller in src/markland/web/app.py passes them. Find where /explore is added and gate it: 'count(public_docs) >= 5'.
2. Update tests/test_robots_sitemap.py + tests/test_sitemap.py: assert /explore is excluded when there are 0 public docs, and included when >= 5.

Effort: 15 min.

Acceptance: /sitemap.xml has 12 URLs (down from 13) until /explore has content; /explore still renders 200 if visited directly.
close_reason: Landed in PR #54; /explore gated behind EXPLORE_MIN_PUBLIC_DOCS=5. sitemap.xml now 12 locs.

title: G1: Reconsider AI search crawler blocks (PerplexityBot + GPTBot)
description:
From 2026-05-03 GEO analysis (docs/audits/2026-05-03-geo-analysis/GEO-ANALYSIS.md, item G1).

Live robots.txt currently blocks both PerplexityBot and GPTBot. Both blocks were inherited from the L3 'AI training crawler' work (PR #48), but they merit separate decisions:

- **PerplexityBot** is search-only (no training exposure). Blocking has no upside; it just hides Markland from Perplexity citations. RECOMMEND: unblock.
- **GPTBot** is dual-use: OpenAI uses it for both training data and the ChatGPT Search index. Blocking is correct if 'no training' is the priority; unblocking gives ChatGPT Search visibility but content may be used for training. DECISION needed.
- For context: ChatGPT-User (live browse), OAI-SearchBot (search-only), and ClaudeBot (Anthropic search) are all already allowed via wildcard. So the site is partly visible already.

Action:
1. Decide on each (PerplexityBot likely yes, GPTBot pending owner judgment).
2. Edit src/markland/web/seo.py to remove the relevant User-agent stanza(s).
3. Update the parametrized test in tests/test_seo_helpers.py to drop the unblocked bots from its assertion list.
4. Verify live robots.txt post-deploy.

Effort: 5 min code once decided.

Acceptance: PerplexityBot (and possibly GPTBot) no longer appear in https://markland.dev/robots.txt; tests pass.
close_reason: Landed in PR #54 (squash bcdec8e); PerplexityBot unblocked. GPTBot stays blocked per owner decision.

title: SECURITY P2: no server-side session revocation (logout is cookie-only)
description:
src/markland/service/sessions.py - signed cookies are stateless; logout deletes the client cookie but the signed token remains valid until exp (30 days). A leaked cookie is hot for a month.

Fix options: server-side sessions table with opaque session_id, OR per-user session_revocation_epoch bumped on logout.

Found by: pre-release multi-agent security review (markland-06e)

title: SECURITY P1: agent token = full account compromise (no per-agent grant scoping)
description:
service/permissions.py:91-106 + _owner_id_for_principal: agents inherit owner-rights on every doc the owning user owns. A leaked agent token grants delete/visibility-flip on all of that user's docs.

Fix: per-agent allowlist or require explicit grant for owner-actions when principal is an agent.

Found by: pre-release multi-agent security review (markland-06e)

title: G2: Add /llms.txt route
description:
From 2026-05-03 GEO analysis (item G2).

llms.txt is the emerging 'golden path map' standard for AI agents (Jeremy Howard / Answer.AI). For an agent-native product, not having one is both a discoverability miss and a brand miss.

A drafted body is in the GEO analysis doc; lift it directly:
- Lead 'Markland' + one-paragraph description
- '## Core' section: /, /quickstart, /alternatives
- '## Per-tool comparisons' section: each /alternatives/{slug}
- '## About' section: /about, /security, /privacy, /terms

Implementation:
1. Add ROUTE in src/markland/web/app.py mirroring /robots.txt:
   @app.get('/llms.txt', response_class=PlainTextResponse)
   def llms_txt(request: Request): return PlainTextResponse(render_llms_txt(base_url))
2. Add render_llms_txt() helper in src/markland/web/seo.py — base_url substituted into each link, same pattern as render_robots_txt.
3. Add tests/test_llms_txt.py: returns 200, content-type text/plain, contains the canonical URLs, escapes properly when base_url has trailing slash.
4. Add 'Sitemap-or-llms.txt' note to docs/ARCHITECTURE.md if relevant.

Effort: 20-30 min.

Acceptance: curl https://markland.dev/llms.txt returns 200 with the agent-readable content map.
close_reason: Landed in PR #54; /llms.txt live at https://markland.dev/llms.txt (200, text/plain).

title: SECURITY P1: CSRF signer falls back to dev-placeholder when secret unset
description:
src/markland/service/sessions.py:134,145 - make_csrf_token/verify_csrf_token signs with literal 'dev-placeholder' when MARKLAND_SESSION_SECRET is empty. _signer correctly raises; CSRF should match that fail-closed posture.

Fix: raise ValueError on empty secret in both make_csrf_token and verify_csrf_token.

Found by: pre-release multi-agent security review (markland-06e)

title: Remove resolve_token legacy fallback path
description: Once 90 days have passed since markland-9dm shipped AND `SELECT COUNT(*) FROM tokens WHERE revoked_at IS NULL AND created_at < '<9dm-deploy-date>'` returns < 5: delete `_resolve_legacy` and the fall-through in `resolve_token`. Keep the parser; new tokens are the only shape by then. Plan: docs/plans/2026-05-04-token-id-prefix-o1-lookup.md

title: P2-A: server-side session revocation epoch
description:
Deferred from PR feat/security-p1p2-fixes (markland-ayv).

The /api/auth/logout endpoint deletes the session cookie but the signed
token remains valid until exp (30 days). To invalidate immediately,
plan: add session_revocation_epoch column on users, embed epoch in
session payload at issue, and reject any cookie whose epoch is older
than the user's current epoch on read_session.

Why deferred: read_session has 13+ callers across web/, all of which
would need a 'conn' argument plumbed through. The blast radius is
larger than the rest of the P1+P2 batch combined and risks late-stage
regressions. Scope this as its own focused PR.

Mitigations in place meanwhile:
- Logout deletes the cookie locally (browser session ends).
- Magic-link verify is single-use so a stolen + already-redeemed token
  can't re-issue a session.
- Compromised secret rotation invalidates all sessions globally.

title: G3: Add question-shaped FAQ sections to /, /quickstart, /alternatives/*
description:
From 2026-05-03 GEO analysis (item G3).

The whole site has only 1 question-shaped H2/H3 across 7 pages (on /quickstart). AI Overviews and ChatGPT Search rank 'X is Y' / 'Why does X Y?' answer-blocks much higher than declarative product copy.

Suggested questions per page:

/ (landing):
- What is Markland?
- How is Markland different from a Git repo?
- Does Markland host my markdown or rewrite it?
- Do readers need an account?

/alternatives/notion:
- Why doesn't Notion work for AI agents?
- Does Markland import from Notion?
- Is Markland a Notion replacement?

/quickstart:
- Do I need an Anthropic account to use Markland?
- Does Markland work with Cursor / Continue / other MCP clients?

Implementation:
1. Add an 'FAQ' section to each landing template — H2 'Frequently asked questions', then H3 per question, each with a 40-80w answer paragraph.
2. Optionally add FAQPage JSON-LD (note: low rich-result lift per Google's 2023 rollback, but AI engines still read mainEntity).
3. Add tests/test_faq_sections.py to assert each question heading is present.

Effort: 1-2 hours (mostly content writing).

Acceptance: 3-4 question-shaped headings on / and 2-3 on each /alternatives/{slug}; tests assert their presence.
close_reason: Landed in PR #54; FAQ on /, /quickstart, all 5 /alternatives/{slug}. 105/105 tests pass.

title: TEST: fix flaky test_read_rejects_tampered_token
description:
tests/test_pending_intent.py::test_read_rejects_tampered_token has been flaky throughout the security-fix sprint (PR #62, PR #64, PR #67). It fails ~10% of full-suite runs with 'DID NOT RAISE InvalidPendingIntent' but always passes on isolated retry.

Likely cause: time-based signature check race (1-second tolerance window?) when the test runs immediately after token signing. Need to read pending_intent.py and the test to confirm.

Fix likely: deterministic time injection via freezegun, or widen the test's tampered payload so the assertion doesn't depend on timing.

Found by: it bit us in CI for PR #67.

title: MCP /mcp path 307-redirects every request, adding ~5-8s to Claude Code session establishment
description:
Production logs from 2026-05-04 show every MCP request from Claude Code's SDK hits a 307 redirect cycle:

  POST /mcp  → 307 → POST /mcp/
  POST /mcp/ → 200

Six redirects observed during a single connection-establishment sequence (~18 seconds total wall time, of which ~5-8s is round-trips eaten by redirects).

Why: FastMCP's streamable_http_app() serves at /mcp/ (trailing slash). The mount in src/markland/web/app.py:200-205 sets:

    mcp_instance.settings.streamable_http_path = "/"

so the sub-app's internal path is just /. Then in app.py:778:

    app.mount("/mcp", mcp_app)

Starlette's mount semantics: a mount point /mcp matches /mcp/* but for /mcp itself (no trailing slash) Starlette issues a 307 redirect to /mcp/. Most Claude Code installs miss the trailing slash because the published Quickstart says https://markland.dev/mcp (no slash).

Fix options (in order of effort):

A. Document the trailing slash in the Quickstart so users register with https://markland.dev/mcp/ from the start. Cheap; no code change. Should ship this immediately as a docs-only patch (republish doc 3366aa58f6ead5e7).

B. Add a redirect-free path: register a route at /mcp (no slash) that proxies to the mounted sub-app, OR mount the sub-app at empty prefix and route /mcp + /mcp/ both to it. Mid effort; needs middleware/PrincipalMiddleware compatibility check (the prefix gate in PrincipalMiddleware uses startswith("/mcp") which still catches both forms — verify before changing).

C. Configure Starlette to NOT redirect on the bare-mount-point case. Look for app.router.redirect_slashes or per-mount equivalent. May not be configurable; if not, fall back to B.

Acceptance:
- POST https://markland.dev/mcp (no slash) returns the same response as POST https://markland.dev/mcp/ (no 307).
- Existing Quickstart users with the trailing slash continue to work.
- PrincipalMiddleware still gates both paths.
- Connection establishment time drops measurably (target: under 8s for a cold connection from a configured client).

Related: markland-2yj (the original install bug), markland-6o6 (this PR's parent — JSON 404 for OAuth probe paths).

title: .dockerignore: prune non-runtime scripts/ artefacts
description: PR #57 ships all of scripts/ to the image including dev-only artefacts (git-hooks/, mcp-config-snippet.json, hosted_smoke.sh). Add .dockerignore to keep COPY scripts /app/scripts simple while excluding noise. Image bloat minimal but cleaner is better.

title: Log magic-link consume rejections at WARN
description: Both /api/auth/verify and /verify currently catch InvalidMagicLink silently. Post single-use enforcement, a replay attempt is a real security signal. Add a single-line _logger.warning("magic-link consume rejected: %s", type(e).__name__) before each HTTPException/HTMLResponse in src/markland/web/auth_routes.py — uses type(e).__name__ to avoid leaking str(e) into logs. Feeds Sentry LoggingIntegration. Out of scope for the original single-use branch (PR feat/magic-link-single-use).

title: Verify GSC indexing health for markland.dev
description:
Three days after GSC sitemap submission (submitted 2026-05-02), verify the Indexing → Pages report has populated and that the sitemap URLs are getting picked up.

Background:
- 2026-05-02: GSC domain property markland.dev verified (TXT record confirmed via dig).
- 2026-05-02: Sitemap https://markland.dev/sitemap.xml submitted successfully (13 URLs).
- 2026-05-02: Homepage URL Inspection showed 'URL is on Google' / 'Page is indexed' ✅.
- 2026-05-02: Indexing → Pages report was still 'Processing data, please check again in a day or so'.
- One-shot Claude reminder also scheduled for Tue 2026-05-05 10:13am local (job f97cc480, session-only — may not survive).

To verify:
1. GSC → Indexing → Pages: capture Indexed vs Not-indexed counts and the top reasons in 'Why pages aren't indexed'.
2. GSC → Sitemaps: confirm 'Discovered URLs' is now > 0 (ideally close to 13).
3. Run 'site:markland.dev' in Google: count visible results, note any 'omitted as similar' clustering.
4. curl -s https://markland.dev/sitemap.xml | grep -c '<loc>' should still return 13.

Acceptance:
- All 13 sitemap URLs are either Indexed or in expected categories (Discovered/Crawled — currently not indexed are normal for a new domain, not a failure).
- No URLs blocked by 'noindex' tag (should be zero).
- No URLs flagged 'Excluded by robots.txt' beyond the intentional /api, /admin, /login, /verify, /setup, /device, /invite, /health, /mcp, /settings, /dashboard, /inbox, /resume.

If clean, flip L5 in docs/audits/2026-04-24-seo-audit/ACTION-PLAN.md status snapshot from open to done.

title: SECURITY P2: defense-in-depth admin gates on docs.feature and audit.list_recent
description:
src/markland/service/docs.py:670-678 feature() comment says 'trusts caller for admin check' - MCP layer enforces but a future HTTP route would inherit no protection.

src/markland/service/audit.py:63-109 list_recent has no principal arg or filter - relies on callers being admin-gated.

Fix: assert principal.is_admin inside both functions as defense-in-depth.

Found by: pre-release multi-agent security review (markland-06e)

title: Soak-window check: verify analytics + admin metrics 2 weeks post-launch
description:
Two weeks after the analytics + admin-metrics launch, verify both pipelines are still healthy and capture a baseline for what 'normal' looks like.

Context: PRs #35 (admin-metrics MCP tool + /admin/metrics endpoint), #37 (Umami drop-in), and #43 (CSP two-host hotfix) all landed 2026-05-01. Production is recording pageviews and admin metrics aggregation works against existing tables. This is the first soak-window check.

Parent issue. Subtasks file the four sub-checks.

title: Pull /admin/metrics 14d funnel snapshot
description:
Query GET /admin/metrics?window_seconds=1209600 (14 days) and capture the funnel:
- signups
- publishes
- grants_created
- invites_accepted
- waitlist_total

Use an admin bearer token; endpoint mirrors /admin/waitlist's auth.

Verify numbers are non-zero and roughly match expected order-of-magnitude after 2 weeks of soft-launch. If everything is zero, there's an aggregation bug or the audit-log action names drifted again (see PR #35 deviation note about 'grant' vs 'grant_create').

Save the JSON output for cross-referencing with the Umami pull.

title: Pull Umami stats for the same 14d window
description:
Query Umami Cloud API for the same 14-day window as the /admin/metrics pull. Capture:
- total visitors / visits / pageviews
- top pages (which surfaces are getting traffic)
- top referrers (where readers are coming from)
- bounce rate

Use UMAMI_API_KEY (already set as a Fly secret) via flyctl ssh exec — DO NOT paste the key into chat. Pattern:

flyctl ssh console -a markland -C 'python3 -c "<script that reads os.environ[UMAMI_API_KEY], hits api.umami.is/v1/websites/{wid}/stats and pageviews and metrics, prints JSON">'

Website ID: 79f6978f-5ade-4019-bee4-46da56d1dd25

title: Cross-reference signup count vs Umami session count
description:
Take signups from /admin/metrics and unique-visitors from Umami over the same 14-day window. Compute the conversion ratio (signups / unique_visitors).

Why: this is the most basic funnel sanity check. If signups vastly exceed unique visitors, attribution is broken (script not loading on landing? bot signups?). If the ratio is plausibly low (single-digit %), the funnel is alive.

Also note any obvious mismatches: if /admin/metrics shows non-zero publishes but Umami shows zero pageviews on /publish or document URLs, the script may be missing on a key surface.

title: Post 1-message summary of soak-window findings
description:
After all three data-pulls and the cross-reference are done, post ONE summary message to the user containing:
- 14-day funnel numbers (signups, publishes, grants, invites, waitlist)
- 14-day traffic numbers (visitors, views, top page, top referrer)
- Conversion ratio (signups / unique_visitors)
- Anomalies found (if any) — empty fields, action-name drift, missing pages, etc.
- One-line verdict: healthy / needs attention / broken

Keep it under 200 words. The user should be able to read it in 30 seconds and know whether to do anything.

title: Provision privacy@markland.dev forwarding alias
description: The /privacy page references mailto:privacy@markland.dev as the contact for data-subject requests (access/deletion/export/breach). Configure the alias to forward to the operator's working inbox before any external party uses it. Source: docs/plans/2026-05-04-formal-privacy-policy.md Task 12.

title: DOC: SameSite=Strict UX side effect for release notes
description:
From PR #64 review (markland-qzo). Signed-in users clicking Markland links from another origin (Slack, email, Twitter) won't have mk_session sent on first request — they appear anonymous on first page load (e.g. /d/{share_token} shows public-doc view stripped of presence identity). After one same-site nav they're authed.

Action: document this in release notes / changelog when announcing public release.

Found by: code review of PR #64.

title: Rename or remove read_magic_link_token to prevent regression
description: After PR #59, no production caller of read_magic_link_token remains — only test and debug usage. It's a footgun: a future contributor could call it on a verify path and silently re-introduce the replay bug. Either (a) delete it and migrate the one test that uses it to consume_magic_link_token, or (b) rename to _read_magic_link_token_unsafe so the danger is obvious in IDEs. Identified during PR #59 review.

title: Presence GC: 'attempt to write a readonly database' on prod (598 events / 9h)
description:
Sentry MARKLAND-1: every 60s tick of presence_gc.gc_expired raises sqlite3.OperationalError 'attempt to write a readonly database' on the new fly machine (7844945bd49568, started 2026-05-04T03:12:26Z). 598 events in 9h, no other write errors visible — GC is the canary because it's the only frequent automatic write.

DB is /data/markland.db (Fly volume vol_rnzwen30xp2kejkr) with PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL. Connection is single, shared, opened once in run_app.py via init_db().

Most likely cause: WAL/SHM file ownership or directory-permission mismatch carried over from a prior machine, or volume at capacity. Same connection used by request handlers — they'd hit the same error if writes were attempted.

Diagnostic commands needed (require prod-read approval):
  flyctl ssh console -C 'ls -la /data/'
  flyctl ssh console -C 'df -h /data && stat /data/markland.db /data/markland.db-wal /data/markland.db-shm'
  flyctl logs --no-tail | grep -iE 'readonly|presence'

Likely fixes:
- chown WAL/SHM to runtime uid then bounce machine
- If full: flyctl volume extend
- Long-term: consider pinning journal_mode=DELETE or writing a tiny self-test write on startup that fails fast and triggers a clear log line
close_reason: Fixed: chown app:app on /data/markland.db{,-wal,-shm} + machine restart. Root cause: PR #64 added USER app to Dockerfile but the existing fly volume DB files were still root-owned. SQLite cached O_RDONLY at open, so chown alone didn't unstick the live process; restart was required.

title: SECURITY P1: Dockerfile runs as root
description:
Dockerfile has no USER directive; container runs as uid 0. /data inherits root ownership.

Fix: RUN useradd -m -u 1000 app && chown -R app:app /data /app && USER app.

Files: /Users/daveyhiles/Developer/markland/Dockerfile

Found by: pre-release multi-agent security review (markland-06e)

title: SECURITY P3: Fly volume mount-as-root vs Dockerfile non-root user
description:
From PR #64 review (markland-l2p). Dockerfile now runs as UID 1000 (good) but Fly mounts /data on first boot owned by root, so app user can't write. Need either runtime chown in start.sh or Fly volume UID config.

Action: smoke-test 'fly deploy' end-to-end (litestream restore + uvicorn DB write) BEFORE declaring P1-D fully done.

Found by: code review of PR #64.

title: Add ClaudeBot to robots.txt AI-crawler blocklist
description:
Code review on PR #48 (https://github.com/dghiles/markland/pull/48) flagged that 'anthropic-ai' and 'Claude-Web' are deprecated user-agents — Anthropic has migrated to 'ClaudeBot' as the active crawler UA. Blocking the legacy names is harmless and matched the audit spec, but to actually catch current Anthropic crawl traffic we should add 'ClaudeBot' (and possibly 'claude-web' lowercase) to the blocklist.

Files:
- src/markland/web/seo.py — add 'User-agent: ClaudeBot\nDisallow: /' stanza
- tests/test_seo_helpers.py — add 'ClaudeBot' to the parametrized test_robots_txt_blocks_ai_training_crawler list

Reference: https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/agents-and-tools/claude-code/overview (Anthropic's bot docs naming)

Acceptance: ClaudeBot stanza renders correctly in /robots.txt and the parametrized test still passes for all entries.
close_reason: Won't-fix / superseded by GEO strategy shift. Original issue proposed adding ClaudeBot to the blocklist (under prior 'block all AI training crawlers' stance). After PR #54/55/56, the strategy is 'AI search visibility wins' — ClaudeBot is now intentionally allowed via wildcard so Markland is citable in Claude. Locked in by test_robots_txt_does_not_block_ai_search_crawler in PR #56 (7de94b1). The deprecated anthropic-ai/Claude-Web stanzas were also dropped in PR #56.

title: Fix stale docstring in rate_limit_middleware.py
description: src/markland/web/rate_limit_middleware.py:69-74 says 'PrincipalMiddleware only runs for /mcp' — no longer true after PR #49 widened it to /mcp + /admin/. Behavior is correct (lazy resolve is idempotent); just the comment lies. One-line doc fix.

title: SECURITY P2: no length caps on markland_publish content/title (storage DoS)
description:
src/markland/server.py:592-620 + service/docs.py:86-126 - docstring explicitly says 'No length limit enforced here.' A single agent token can write unbounded blobs, exhausting SQLite.

Fix: enforce len(content) <= ~1MB and len(title) <= 500 at tool boundary; return invalid_argument.

Found by: pre-release multi-agent security review (markland-06e)

title: SECURITY P0: magic-link tokens leak to access logs and Sentry
description:
GET /verify?token=... lands in uvicorn access log (default on, no scrub) and Sentry breadcrumbs. JTI single-use mitigates replay-after-consume but window between email send and verify is exploitable.

Fixes:
1. uvicorn access-log filter that strips token=, share_token=, csrf= query params
2. sentry-sdk before_send that drops Authorization header and rewrites those query params
3. Consider POST/cookie-handoff for magic-link verify to keep token off the URL entirely

Files: src/markland/run_app.py:51 (sentry init), src/markland/run_app.py:96 (uvicorn config), src/markland/web/auth_routes.py:152 (GET /verify)

Found by: pre-release multi-agent security review (markland-06e)

title: SECURITY P1: CSRF gap on session-authed mutating routes
description:
State-changing browser POSTs rely on SameSite=Lax only:
- POST /api/tokens, DELETE /api/tokens/{id}
- POST/DELETE /api/agents/*, /settings/agents/create|delete|tokens/create
- POST /api/auth/logout
- POST /d/{t}/fork, /d/{t}/bookmark
- POST /api/invites/{t}/accept
- POST /api/docs/{id}/grants

Existing make_csrf_token/verify_csrf_token (service/sessions.py) is wired only on /device/confirm.

Fix options:
A. Widen CSRF token to all session-authed mutating routes (more work)
B. Upgrade session cookie to SameSite=Strict (low-effort; acceptable since signed-in flows are internal)

Found by: pre-release multi-agent security review (markland-06e)

title: scripts/admin/lookup_doc_grants.py — retire last inline python -c snippet
description: docs/runbooks/admin-operations.md:181 still has an inline 'python -c' for grants-on-a-doc lookup. Add scripts/admin/lookup_doc_grants.py <doc_id> and replace the inline form. Reviewer flagged in PR #57.

title: Provision legal@markland.dev forwarding alias
description: The /terms page references mailto:legal@markland.dev as the contact for ToS-related notices. Configure the alias to forward to the operator's working inbox before any external party uses it. Source: docs/plans/2026-05-04-formal-terms-of-service.md Task 15.

title: G4: Add 134-167-word "What is Markland?" answer block to /
description:
From 2026-05-03 GEO analysis (item G4). Current / opens with 'Stop copy-pasting your agent's work.' — punchy for humans, useless as an AI citation. AI Overviews preferentially cite blocks in the 134-167 word range that lead with 'X is Y'. Drop in a new

What is Markland?

section right after the H1 hero, with a ~140-word self-contained answer paragraph. Existing punchy copy can stay below it. A draft passage is in docs/audits/2026-05-03-geo-analysis/GEO-ANALYSIS.md under 'Content Reformatting Suggestions'. Implementation: 1. Edit src/markland/web/templates/landing.html. 2. Add the section between the H1/hero and the existing 'Stop copy-pasting' lead. 3. Refresh meta description if needed (current is 160 chars — already at the limit). 4. Add a test that asserts: page contains 'What is Markland?' h2 + the new paragraph contains the words 'markdown', 'agent', 'MCP'. Effort: 30 min. Acceptance: AI Overviews and ChatGPT Search definition queries for 'markland' return the new answer block.
close_reason: Landed in PR #54; 143-word 'What is Markland?' answer block above the hero on /.

title: SECURITY P3: HSTS max-age bump to 1y + preload (post-canonical-domain)
description:
src/markland/web/security_headers_middleware.py:78 - currently 86400s, comment acknowledges intentional pre-canonical-domain. Bump to 31536000 + preload once markland.dev is stable.

Found by: pre-release multi-agent security review (markland-06e)

title: SECURITY P3: extend admin gate to audit.list_recent_paginated
description:
markland-ezu (PR #64) added defense-in-depth admin gate to audit.list_recent but missed list_recent_paginated. The MCP tool path uses _paginated and gates at the tool layer; if a future caller wires _paginated somewhere new and forgets the gate, no DiD catches it.

Fix: add same principal=None / is_admin gate to list_recent_paginated in service/audit.py.

Found by: code review of PR #64.

title: Claude Code SDK 'Authenticate' menu doesn't surface bearer-token config path for non-OAuth MCP servers
description:
When Claude Code's MCP SDK probes a server that returns clean 'no OAuth' signals (per RFC 9728 / our markland-2yj + markland-6o6 fixes), the UI panel reports:

  Status: ◯ connecting…
  Auth:   ✘ not authenticated
  SDK auth failed: Markland does not run an OAuth authorization server or
  OIDC provider. Use a static bearer token minted at https://markland.dev/settings/tokens.

  ❯ 1. Authenticate
    2. Reconnect
    3. Disable

The error message is correct and actionable — it tells the user exactly what to do (mint a token, configure bearer auth). But the menu offers no path to do it. "Authenticate" likely re-attempts OAuth (which doesn't exist), "Reconnect" tries again with the same broken config, "Disable" turns off the server.

A user with no bearer token in ~/.claude.json is stuck in this loop unless they know to:
  1. Quit Claude Code
  2. claude mcp remove markland
  3. Visit /settings/tokens, mint a token
  4. claude mcp add markland --transport http https://markland.dev/mcp/ \
       --header "Authorization: Bearer mk_usr_<token>"
  5. Restart Claude Code

This is an upstream Claude Code SDK UX issue — we can't fix it server-side. Tracking here so:
  (a) we don't lose the observation, and
  (b) when the install/onboarding Options 2-4 plan
      (docs/plans/2026-05-04-install-onboarding-options-2-4.md) ships, we
      verify it's the right workaround for this exact menu state.

What WOULD make this self-service:
  - A "4. Edit config" menu option that opens ~/.claude.json with a
    template hint for the headers field.
  - Or: when the resource_metadata document declares
    'bearer-token-only' (per RFC 9728), the SDK should prompt the user
    inline for the token rather than presenting OAuth-shaped options.

Filed in response to a real-user install attempt 2026-05-04. Workaround
documented in the install/onboarding plan above; landing Phase 1 of that
plan is the user-facing closure for this issue (the /setup runbook will
walk Claude Code through device flow and produce the correct
'claude mcp add ... --header' command end-to-end).

Sibling issues:
  - markland-2yj (closed) — WWW-Authenticate header + well-known JSON
  - markland-6o6 (closed) — JSON 404 on every observed probe path
  - markland-dfj (open) — /mcp 307-redirect cycle on every request

Reference: see screenshot in conversation 2026-05-04 11:24 EDT.

title: SECURITY P3: dedupe invites by (doc_id, target_email) on _grant_via_invite
description:
From PR #64 fixup review. _grant_via_invite calls create_invite unconditionally, so re-granting the same unknown email creates duplicate invite rows. Only visible to doc owner — not an attacker oracle — but hygiene issue.

Fix: dedupe by (doc_id, target_email_hash) — return existing invite if one exists.

Found by: code review of PR #64 fixup.

title: Equalize magic-link verify timing across failure modes
description: Low-severity timing side channel: replay path executes ~2 extra SQL statements (DELETE + INSERT OR IGNORE + commit) before raising, while bad-signature/expired raise immediately. A patient attacker timing many replays could in principle distinguish 'valid signature, already-used JTI' from 'bad signature'. Threat model is narrow (attacker already has a valid token) but worth closing. Mitigation: either run GC+INSERT for all signature-valid tokens regardless of further payload checks, or add a short uniform sleep on early-raise paths. Identified during PR #59 review.

title: Establish LinkedIn presence + add to Organization.sameAs
description:
From SEO strategy (Appendix C item 4). Brand-mention surface for AI citations.

Brand mentions on LinkedIn correlate ~moderately with AI-citation visibility (per the GEO analysis Ahrefs Dec 2025 study). Today: zero LinkedIn footprint.

Steps:
1. Decide: personal LinkedIn page vs Markland company page vs both. Recommend BOTH but personal first; the company page is empty noise until there's something to post about it.
2. Personal LinkedIn:
   - Update headline to mention building Markland (e.g., 'Building Markland — agent-native publishing for AI agents | ex-X')
   - Add Markland as a project under Experience
   - Post one substantive piece (e.g., 'I built a tool because of this specific problem...')
3. Markland company page (after personal is set up):
   - Logo, tagline, description matching the site's
   - Link to https://markland.dev
   - Initial post: cross-post of the launch announcement / the first anchor blog post
4. Site updates:
   - Add LinkedIn URL to Organization.sameAs in src/markland/web/templates/_seo_meta.html
   - Add to Person.sameAs on /about/dghiles (after author page exists — see sibling bead)
   - Footer: optional LinkedIn icon next to GitHub (may or may not be worth the visual weight)
5. Test: tests/test_seo_meta.py asserts LinkedIn URL appears in Organization.sameAs.

No urgency — this can wait until you actually feel ready to post on LinkedIn, since an empty profile linked from your site is worse than no link at all.

Effort: 30 min site changes + however long the LinkedIn page-building takes (~1-2 hours including writing one decent post).

title: Phase 2.1: /blog infrastructure (route + Article schema + Person author + RSS)
description:
From SEO strategy (docs/audits/2026-05-03-seo-strategy/SEO-STRATEGY.md, Appendix C item 1).

Ship the /blog infrastructure as a prerequisite for the 6 anchor blog posts in Phase 2.

Scope:
1. FastAPI route at /blog (index) and /blog/{slug} (post detail) in src/markland/web/app.py.
2. Markdown rendering using the existing pipeline (same stack as /d/{token} viewer — markdown-it-py + Pygments). New posts live in src/markland/web/content/blog/{slug}.md with frontmatter (title, slug, published_at, updated_at, author, description, og_image).
3. Article JSON-LD per post: @type=Article (NOT BlogPosting unless the publication cadence calls for it), with author (Person), datePublished, dateModified, headline, image, mainEntityOfPage, publisher (link to Organization).
4. Person schema for the author — first-class, separate from any in-page Organization.founder block. {name, url, jobTitle, sameAs: [github, linkedin once exists]}. Reusable as the founder Person across the site (closes the docs/FOLLOW-UPS.md 'Organization.founder real name vs handle' item).
5. RSS feed at /blog/feed.xml — single FastAPI route returning Atom or RSS 2.0 from the same posts list. Cache headers should match /sitemap.xml (no-cache or short max-age).
6. Update /llms.txt template to add a '## Blog' section once the first post ships (or right away if the route exists with zero posts).
7. Update sitemap to include /blog and /blog/{slug} (gated like /explore behind 'has at least 1 published post').
8. Tests:
   - tests/test_blog.py: route returns 200, content-type text/html, contains H1/Article schema, OG tags
   - tests/test_blog_rss.py: feed validates as RSS or Atom (use feedparser)
   - tests/test_blog_sitemap.py: /blog/{slug} URLs included once posts exist
9. CSS rhythm matching the rest of the site (Figtree display font, max-width 760px, line-height 1.65). Reuse landing.html .answer-block style.

Effort: 4-6 hours total.

Acceptance:
- curl https://markland.dev/blog → 200 (empty state OK)
- curl https://markland.dev/blog/feed.xml → 200 valid feed
- All tests green
- /llms.txt includes blog entry
- Person schema renders correctly when shared on Slack/Twitter (validate with the OG inspector)
close_reason: Shipped in PR #63 (squash c9a6c16). /blog index, /blog/{slug}, /blog/feed.xml all live. Sitemap + llms.txt gated on ≥1 published post (mirrors EXPLORE_MIN_PUBLIC_DOCS pattern). Article + Person + BreadcrumbList JSON-LD per post. 31 new tests, full suite 1046 passing.

title: SECURITY P0: stored XSS via javascript: link scheme in markdown
description:
markdown-it renders [text](javascript:alert(1)) without scheme validation. Stored XSS on every doc viewer.

Fix: add custom link validator rejecting non-http(s)/mailto schemes, or run output through bleach/nh3 with allowlist.

Files: src/markland/web/renderer.py
Found by: pre-release multi-agent security review (markland-06e)

title: SECURITY P2: grant-target email enumeration via GrantTargetNotFound
description:
src/markland/service/grants.py:60-66 + web/api_grants.py:153-161 - POST /api/docs/{id}/grants returns distinct error when email is unknown to Markland. Doc owner can enumerate which arbitrary emails belong to accounts.

Fix: for owner+nonexistent-target, silently send invite email or return generic 'ok / pending'.

Found by: pre-release multi-agent security review (markland-06e)

title: SECURITY P2: CSP allows unsafe-inline for script-src
description:
src/markland/web/security_headers_middleware.py:60-65 - script-src 'unsafe-inline' negates the primary defense-in-depth for any XSS sink. Multiple templates have inline <script> blocks.

Fix: per-request nonce in middleware (or move all inline scripts to /assets/*.js), drop 'unsafe-inline' from script-src.

Found by: pre-release multi-agent security review (markland-06e)
